passage.title: Bracelet Pattern Test
passage.text: Our craft club tested two ways to make bead bracelets last month. Mina used a long written list, but Joon used a color pattern card. After one week, Joon made fewer mistakes because the pattern card showed the colors clearly. The club counted each finished bracelet and checked the color pattern. When Mina saw the difference, she changed to a color pattern card. At the next club meeting, she also finished with fewer errors. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that clear patterns can help with a craft project.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Learning a clearer way to make bead bracelets
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Joon use to make the bracelet?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does pattern mean in the passage?
choices: A design that follows the same order
A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Mina switch to a color pattern card?
choices: She thought the colors would be easier to follow
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
